papers.title: Informal Transit and the Future City: Integrating Matatus, Trotros, and Minibuses into Formal Networks
papers.summary: Examining how 14 African cities are formalising informal transport operators — and which regulatory models best balance operator livelihoods with passenger safety and network efficiency.

papers.title: E-Mobility Leapfrogging: Can Africa Skip Fossil-Fuel Transit and Go Straight to Electric?
papers.summary: Assessing the infrastructure, financing, and policy conditions under which African countries could bypass combustion-engine mass transit and deploy electric BRT and rail as a first-generation formal network.

papers.title: BRT in Africa: A Review of Outcomes Across 12 Rapid Transit Systems
papers.summary: A systematic review of bus rapid transit performance across Lagos, Dar es Salaam, Johannesburg, and nine other African cities, assessing ridership, equity, and financial sustainability.

papers.title: Walking Cities: The Case for Pedestrian Infrastructure Investment in Sub-Saharan Africa
papers.summary: A multi-city study quantifying the economic and health value of pedestrian infrastructure across Kampala, Accra, Addis Ababa, and Lusaka — and the policy levers most effective at shifting investment priorities.

papers.title: Boda Boda, Okada, and Bajaj: Motorcycle Taxis and the Road Safety Challenge in East and West Africa
papers.summary: An evaluation of motorcycle taxi operations across eight cities, measuring their role in last-mile connectivity against the road-safety, regulatory, and emissions trade-offs of formalisation.

papers.title: Electric Two- and Three-Wheelers: Africa's Most Viable Path to Clean Urban Mobility?
papers.summary: Survey and market analysis examining the adoption potential of electric motorcycles and auto-rickshaws across 10 African cities, exploring consumer attitudes, charging infrastructure needs, and financing barriers.
